name: Brett Storoe
title: President
role: Overall Management of Events & Wider-Community Outreach
bio: Hi! I'm Brett Storoe, and I'm currently a Sophomore Computer Science student. I joined AI Club to explore the latest AI technologies and acquire the skills to apply them. I've learned a lot through AI Club, and I'm excited to see the club continue to grow! Outside of AI Club, I enjoy thrifting and listening to music.

name: Andrew Needham
title: Vice President
role: Internal Organizational Maintenance & Lead Workshop Developer
bio: Hi! My name is Andrew Needham, and I am a Sophomore Computer Science student at MSOE. I joined AI club as a freshman to learn more about machine learning and other technology, and I have had lots of fun participating in all the different events. I'm also part of the Raider Robotics VEXU team, and I enjoy game development and downhill skiing.

name: Adam Swedlund
title: Campus Outreach
bio: Hi! I'm Adam Swedlund, a sophomore studying Software Engineering. I joined the AI Club to expand my understanding of AI and explore its real-world applications. In my free time, I enjoy photography and exploring the Milwaukee area with friends.

name: Madison Engebose
title: Technical Strategist
bio: Hi! I'm Madison Engebose, and I'm currently a Sophomore Computer Science student at MSOE. I joined AI Club to explore different AI concepts and develop the skills to apply them in real-world scenarios. Outside of AI club, I'm a Software Engineer Intern for Direct Supply and a member of the MSOE Women's Golf team.

name: Dr. Riley
title: Faculty Advisor
role: Expert in Machine/Deep Learning, AI, Simulation, and High-Performance Computing.
bio: Dr. Derek Riley joined the MSOE faculty in 2016 and is a professor in the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science Department. He is also program director of MSOE's Bachelor of Science in Computer Science program, which has a focus in artificial intelligence. In addition to teaching at MSOE, Riley provides consulting services and expert witness services related to machine learning, deep learning, facial recognition, computational modeling, high-performance computing, and other related fields. His areas of expertise include deep learning, machine learning, computer vision, algorithms, process modeling and simulation, Scrum, and mobile computing/programming. He is an NVIDIA DLI Certified Instructor.
